commonProblems.h2: Volkswagen Touran Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Volkswagen Touran's 1.9/2.0 TDI diesels and 1.4 TSI petrols dominate UK replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the typical failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this decision tool to compare repair vs replacement costs.

problems.h4: 2.0 TDI Porous Cylinder Head - Why BKD/AZV Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Touran SE, SEL, Cross (2003-2010, BKD/AZV 2.0 TDI 140hp)
problems.rootCause: Casting porosity in cylinder head allows coolant to seep into combustion chambers, causing overheating and potential head gasket failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, machine surface, replace gasket
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; porosity remains, failure likely to recur

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit reconditioned cylinder head with new valves/seals
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses root cause but labour-intensive; engine may have other wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit fully rebuilt long-block with upgraded components
repairOptions.longevity: Most cost-effective for high-mileage Tourans; includes 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: On a Touran worth £3,000-£6,000, the £3,000 cylinder head repair rarely makes financial sense. A reconditioned BKD engine from £1,500 offers better value and peace of mind with warranty coverage.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt BKD engine prices for your Volkswagen Touran

problems.h4: 1.4 TSI Timing Chain Failure - Why EA111 Engines Rattle on Startup
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Touran SE, Match, GT (2006-2015, BMY/CAVC/BLG 1.4 TSI 140-170hp)
problems.rootCause: Original timing chain tensioner design fails to maintain tension, allowing chain to skip teeth and cause catastrophic valve/piston damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, tensioner, guides before failure
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if caught early with rattle; no guarantee against future issues

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EA111 with upgraded tensioner and piston rings
repairOptions.longevity: Only reliable solution once damage occurs; includes warranty protection

problems.recommendation: If you hear a rattle on cold start, act immediately. Once the chain has jumped, engine damage is usually terminal. For most owners, a rebuilt unit from £1,200 is the only sensible path.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt BMY CAVC engine prices for your Volkswagen Touran

problems.h4: 1.9 TDI Injector Failure - Why BXE Engines Misfire and Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Touran SE, Match, Life (2003-2010, BXE/BLS 1.9 TDI 105hp)
problems.rootCause: Piezo injector internal failure causes fuel delivery errors, leading to misfires, rough idle, and potential piston damage from unburnt fuel.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace one faulty injector with coding
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary; other injectors likely to fail soon after

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all four injectors with calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses immediate issue but engine may have other high-mileage wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 1.9 TDI with new injectors fitted
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for older Tourans; includes warranty and peace of mind

problems.recommendation: The £2,000 injector set repair often exceeds the value of a 2003-2006 Touran. A reconditioned BKC/BXE engine from £800 is the smarter financial decision.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt BKC BXE engine prices for your Volkswagen Touran

problems.h4: 1.6 TDI Turbo Failure - Why CAYC Engines Lose Power at 95k Miles
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Touran Match, SE, BlueMotion (2010-2015, CAYC 1.6 TDI 105hp)
problems.rootCause: Turbocharger bearing wear allows oil leakage into intake/exhaust, causing smoke, power loss, and potential engine damage from oil starvation.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, refurbish, and refit original turbo
repairOptions.longevity: Cost-effective if caught early; no warranty on rebuilt turbo alone

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt CAYC engine with new turbo fitted
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution; includes full engine warranty, not just turbo

problems.recommendation: Turbo failure on the CAYC often indicates broader engine wear. For a 2010-2015 Touran, a rebuilt engine from £900 with warranty offers better long-term value than a turbo-only fix.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt CAYC engine prices for your Volkswagen Touran

problems.h4: DSG Mechatronic Failure - Why Automatic Tourans Hesitate and Slip
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Touran SE, SEL, Match DSG (2003-2015, all DSG-equipped variants)
problems.rootCause: Mechatronic unit solenoid wear or electrical faults cause erratic gear selection, hesitation, and eventual gearbox lock-up.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Repair or replace mechatronic unit only
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses symptoms but clutch wear may still require attention

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit reconditioned DSG gearbox with warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution; includes clutch pack and mechatronic coverage

problems.recommendation: DSG repairs are complex. If your Touran has high mileage, consider whether investing in gearbox repair makes sense versus replacing the entire vehicle. For engine-related DSG searches, we can quote engine + DSG packages.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Volkswagen Touran DSG and engine replacement

problems.h4: EGR Valve & Intake Manifold Failure - Why All Touran Diesels Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Touran all diesel variants (2003-2023, all TDI engine codes)
problems.rootCause: EGR valve coking restricts exhaust flow; plastic intake manifold cracks cause air leaks, triggering limp mode and power loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Clean or blank EGR, replace manifold gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; cracks may recur, EGR will re-coke

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit new intake manifold and EGR valve assembly
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses immediate issues but engine may have other high-mileage wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new manifold/EGR fitted
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when multiple systems failing; includes full warranty coverage

problems.recommendation: EGR and manifold issues are often symptoms of broader engine wear on high-mileage diesels. If your Touran has multiple fault codes, a rebuilt engine may be more cost-effective than piecemeal repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt diesel engine prices for your Volkswagen Touran

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Volkswagen Touran
finalCta.paragraph: The problems above represent the most common reasons UK Touran owners search for replacement engines. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Volkswagen Touran specialists in minutes. Every rebuilt and reconditioned engine includes upgraded components addressing the typical failure point, bench testing, and a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Get back on the road without the main dealer premium - compare quotes today.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Volkswagen Touran Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Volkswagen Touran and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Volkswagen Touran Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Volkswagen Touran is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify your unit.

items.title: Volkswagen Touran 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.6 TDI BlueMotion engine arrived with the 5T generation in 2010, offering improved economy but introducing turbo reliability concerns that now drive replacement enquiries for 2010-2015 models.
commonFailurePoints: Turbocharger failure around 95k miles; injector seal leaks
items.cta: Get quotes for Volkswagen Touran 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Volkswagen Touran 1.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The workhorse 1.9 TDI PD powered early Tourans from launch; generally robust but injector failures on BXE-coded units and DMF wear from 60k miles are common replacement triggers.
commonFailurePoints: Injector failure (BXE); dual-mass flywheel wear from 60k miles
items.cta: Get quotes for Volkswagen Touran 1.9L diesel engines

items.title: Volkswagen Touran 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0 TDI dominates Touran replacement demand; BKD/AZV porous cylinder heads and BMN turbo failures are the most costly and frequently searched failure patterns across 1T and 5T generations.
commonFailurePoints: Porous cylinder head (BKD/AZV); turbo failure (BMN/CAYC); EGR/intake manifold cracks
items.cta: Get quotes for Volkswagen Touran 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Volkswagen Touran 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The downsized 1.2 TSI arrived with the 5T generation, offering adequate performance but sharing EA111 family concerns around timing components on early production units.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner wear; carbon buildup on intake valves
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Touran 1.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volkswagen Touran 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.4 TSI EA111 family is the most problematic Touran petrol engine; timing chain tensioner failure causes catastrophic damage, making rebuilt replacement the only reliable solution once rattles appear.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner failure (80k+ miles); excessive oil consumption from piston ring wear
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Touran 1.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volkswagen Touran 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The naturally aspirated 1.6 FSI and MPI engines offer simplicity over the turbo units; carbon buildup on FSI intake valves is the primary concern, with fewer catastrophic failure patterns.
commonFailurePoints: Carbon buildup on FSI intake valves; ignition coil failures on MPI units
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Touran 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volkswagen Touran 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0 FSI provided stronger performance for early Tourans but shares the carbon buildup vulnerability of the smaller FSI units; timing chain guide wear can occur on very high-mileage examples.
commonFailurePoints: Intake valve carbon buildup; timing chain guide wear on high mileage
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Touran 2.0L petrol engine prices

groups.title: Volkswagen Touran Hybrid / CNG Engine Sizes

items.title: Volkswagen Touran 1.4L CNG Engines
items.description: The EcoFuel 1.4 TSI CNG variant offered dual-fuel flexibility but remains rare in the UK; replacement enquiries typically relate to standard petrol-mode timing chain concerns rather than CNG-specific issues.
commonFailurePoints: CNG injector seal degradation; standard EA111 timing chain vulnerability
items.cta: Enquire about Volkswagen Touran 1.4L hybrid engine availability

items.title: Volkswagen Touran 2.0L CNG Engines
items.description: The larger EcoFuel 2.0 CNG was even rarer than the 1.4; replacement considerations mirror the standard 2.0 FSI with added attention to CNG fuel system components.
commonFailurePoints: regulator wear; standard FSI carbon buildup
items.cta: Enquire about Volkswagen Touran 2.0L hybrid engine availability

fuelTypes.h2: Volkswagen Touran Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Volkswagen Touran engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early 1.9 TDI to the latest EA288 2.0 TDI.

items.title: Volkswagen Touran Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel engines powered most UK Tourans across all generations, with the 1.9/2.0 TDI units dominating replacement enquiries due to documented failure patterns.
items.descriptor: Diesel engines powered most UK Tourans across all generations, with the 1.9/2.0 TDI units dominating replacement enquiries due to documented failure patterns.
items.familiesLabel: Common Volkswagen Touran Diesel Engine Families
families: BKD/AZV - 2.0 TDI 140hp (1T), porous cylinder head failures from 150k km
BKC/BXE - 1.9 TDI 105hp (1T), injector failures on BXE-coded units
CAYC - 1.6 TDI 105hp (5T), turbo failure documented at ~95k miles
CRVA/CYKB - 2.0 TDI 150hp EA288 (5T facelift), improved but injector issues reported
foundIn: 1T generation (2003-2010) - BKD, AZV, BKC, BLS, BXE, BMN, BMM
knownFor: Strong motorway fuel economy and towing capability
BKD porous cylinder head failures driving £3,000 repair costs
High UK replacement demand on 1.9/2.0 TDI units over 100k miles
DPF/EGR issues on short-journey vehicles causing limp mode
items.cta: Get quotes for Volkswagen Touran Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Volkswagen Touran Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol Tourans offered simpler maintenance but the 1.4 TSI EA111 family's timing chain vulnerability creates urgent replacement needs for affected owners.
items.descriptor: Petrol Tourans offered simpler maintenance but the 1.4 TSI EA111 family's timing chain vulnerability creates urgent replacement needs for affected owners.
items.familiesLabel: Common Volkswagen Touran Petrol Engine Families
families: BMY/CAVC - 1.4 TSI 140hp EA111, timing chain tensioner failure risk
BLG/CAVB - 1.4 TSI 170hp high-output, same EA111 chain concerns
BAG/BSE - 1.6 FSI/MPI naturally aspirated, carbon buildup on FSI variants
BJZ - 2.0 FSI 150hp, larger displacement with similar carbon issues
foundIn: 1T generation (2003-2010) - BAG, BLF, BSE, BSF, BJZ, BMY, BLG
knownFor: Smoother, quieter operation than diesel equivalents
1.4 TSI timing chain failures causing catastrophic engine damage
FSI variants prone to intake valve carbon buildup requiring walnut blasting
Lower replacement costs for naturally aspirated 1.6 MPI units

items.description: Rare EcoFuel variants offered dual-fuel flexibility but remain specialist replacements with limited supplier availability in the UK network.
items.descriptor: Rare EcoFuel variants offered dual-fuel flexibility but remain specialist replacements with limited supplier availability in the UK network.
families: BSX - 2.0 EcoFuel 109hp (2009-2010, very rare UK specification)
Compressed natural gas stored in underfloor tanks supplements petrol operation
Automatic switching between fuel types based on availability and load
Petrol-mode operation shares standard EA111/FSI failure patterns
importantNotes: CNG injector and regulator components require specialist knowledge
Replacement engine availability more limited than standard petrol/diesel units
Verify CNG tank certification status when purchasing used EcoFuel Touran

engineYears.h2: Volkswagen Touran Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Volkswagen Touran's production run from 2003 to 2023, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The original Touran launched with proven 1.9 TDI and simple petrol units, establishing the MPV formula that would define the model's UK success.
keyChanges: Volkswagen Touran launched in UK as compact 7-seat MPV based on Golf Mk5 platform
Initial engine lineup: 1.6 FSI, 2.0 FSI petrol; 1.9 TDI, 2.0 TDI diesel
Introduction of PD (Pumpe-Düse) diesel technology across range
knownFor: Spacious 7-seat layout ideal for families
BKD 2.0 TDI porous cylinder head issues emerge after 150k km
1.9 TDI BKC regarded as most reliable early diesel option
Simple naturally aspirated petrol engines easy to maintain
years.cta: Get quotes for 2003-2006 Volkswagen Touran replacement engines
years.ticker: Volkswagen Touran 2.0 TDI coolant loss • Volkswagen Touran 1.9 TDI injector failure • Volkswagen Touran 1.6 FSI carbon buildup

years.description: The 2006 facelift introduced the problematic 1.4 TSI EA111 petrol engine while refining diesel options, creating two distinct replacement demand profiles.
keyChanges: Introduction of 1.4 TSI EA111 turbo petrol engines (BMY, BLG)
Facelift exterior styling and interior trim updates
DSG automatic gearbox availability expanded across range
knownFor: 1.4 TSI timing chain tensioner failures begin appearing at 80k+ miles
BXE-coded 1.9 TDI injector failures drive replacement enquiries
BMN 170hp turbo failures on high-mileage Cross Touran models
Cross Touran raised suspension variant gains cult following
years.cta: Get quotes for 2006-2010 Volkswagen Touran replacement engines
years.ticker: Volkswagen Touran 1.4 TSI rattle on startup • Volkswagen Touran BXE injector misfire • Volkswagen Touran BMN turbo whistle
enquiries: Volkswagen Touran 1.4 TSI rattle on startup

years.description: The all-new 5T generation brought downsized engines including the 1.6 TDI BlueMotion and updated EA111 petrols, with new failure patterns emerging.
keyChanges: All-new 5T platform with improved interior space and refinement
Introduction of 1.2 TSI and 1.6 TDI BlueMotion engines
Common-rail diesel technology replaces PD across range
knownFor: CAYC 1.6 TDI turbo failures documented at ~95k miles
EA111 1.4 TSI timing chain concerns continue on CAVC/CAVB codes
BlueMotion variants popular for company car tax efficiency
Improved refinement but complex emissions systems increase repair costs
years.cta: Get quotes for 2010-2015 Volkswagen Touran replacement engines
years.ticker: Volkswagen Touran 1.6 TDI loss of power • Volkswagen Touran CAVC oil consumption • Volkswagen Touran 1.2 TSI timing noise
enquiries: Volkswagen Touran 1.6 TDI loss of power

years.description: The final facelift introduced the cleaner EA288 diesel family and updated infotainment, with improved reliability but higher replacement costs for late-model units.
keyChanges: Introduction of EA288 modular diesel engine family (CRVA, CYKB)
Updated LED lighting and Discover Media infotainment
Enhanced driver assistance systems and safety equipment
mainEngines: DFEB/DFGB - 2.0 TDI EA288 diesel (190hp, rare UK)
Updated 1.4 TSI EA211 (not widely fitted to UK Touran)
knownFor: EA288 engines generally more reliable but injector issues reported on early units
Higher complexity increases specialist repair costs
Late-model Tourans retain strong residual values affecting repair vs replace decisions
Final production years see reduced UK registration numbers
years.cta: Get quotes for 2015-2023 Volkswagen Touran replacement engines
years.ticker: Volkswagen Touran CRVA injector fault • Volkswagen Touran EA288 EGR cooler • Volkswagen Touran 2020 engine replacement

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Volkswagen Touran was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Volkswagen Touran specialists.

faq.h2: Volkswagen Touran Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Volkswagen Touran engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Volkswagen Touran engine typically costs £800-£3,500 supply-only depending on engine code and variant, with fitting adding £400-£800. Used engines start from £500 but carry shorter warranties.
keyPoints: 1.9 TDI BKC rebuilt from £800, 2.0 TDI BKD from £1,500
1.4 TSI EA111 rebuilt from £1,200 due to complexity
Supply & fit service available nationwide through network garages
All rebuilt units include minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Touran engine replacement prices now

items.question: Which Volkswagen Touran engine is most reliable?
items.answer: The 1.9 TDI BKC/BLS codes are widely regarded as the most reliable Volkswagen Touran engines, offering robust PD diesel technology with fewer catastrophic failure patterns than later units.
keyPoints: BKC 1.9 TDI 105hp praised for longevity with proper maintenance
Avoid BXE-coded 1.9 TDI due to injector failure risk
Naturally aspirated 1.6 MPI petrol (BSE/BSF) also very reliable
EA288 2.0 TDI (CRVA) shows improved reliability but limited long-term data
items.cta: Find reliable Volkswagen Touran engine options

items.question: What causes Volkswagen Touran 2.0 TDI BKD engines to lose coolant?
items.answer: Porous cylinder head casting on BKD/AZV 2.0 TDI engines allows coolant to seep into combustion chambers, causing overheating, white smoke and potential head gasket failure from around 150,000 km.
keyPoints: Porosity is a manufacturing defect, not maintenance-related
Symptoms include coolant loss without visible leaks, overheating, white exhaust smoke
Cylinder head replacement costs ~£3,000, often exceeding vehicle value
Rebuilt BKD engine from £1,500 offers better value with warranty
items.cta: Get quotes for BKD Volkswagen Touran engine replacement

items.question: Is the Volkswagen Touran 1.4 TSI timing chain problem fixable?
items.answer: The EA111 1.4 TSI timing chain tensioner failure on BMY/CAVC/BLG engines cannot be reliably prevented once rattling starts; catastrophic valve damage usually requires full engine replacement.
keyPoints: Rattle on cold start indicates imminent tensioner failure
Preventative chain kit replacement only viable before symptoms appear
Once damage occurs, rebuilt engine from £1,200 is only reliable solution
Upgraded tensioner fitted to all rebuilt EA111 units through our network
items.cta: Compare rebuilt 1.4 TSI Volkswagen Touran engine prices

items.question: Can I fit a Golf engine in my Volkswagen Touran?
items.answer: Yes, most Volkswagen Touran engines are shared with contemporary Golf, Jetta and Octavia models, making donor sourcing easier and keeping replacement costs competitive across the VW Group platform.
keyPoints: BKD, BKC, CAYC, BMY codes all fitted to Golf Mk5/Mk6
Verify exact engine code and ECU compatibility before purchase
Platform sharing means specialist knowledge widely available
Always confirm mounting points and ancillary compatibility for your variant
items.cta: Check Volkswagen Touran engine compatibility options

items.question: How long should a Volkswagen Touran engine last?
items.answer: With proper maintenance, Volkswagen Touran diesel engines can reach 150,000-200,000 miles, but known failure points like BKD porous heads or EA111 timing chains often require intervention before this mileage.
keyPoints: 1.9 TDI BKC most likely to reach high mileage with regular servicing
2.0 TDI BKD vulnerable to porous head issues from 93k miles
1.4 TSI EA111 timing chain risk from 80k miles regardless of maintenance
Rebuilt engines reset the mileage clock with warranty protection
items.cta: Get Volkswagen Touran engine longevity advice

items.question: What's the difference between reconditioned and rebuilt Volkswagen Touran engines?
items.answer: Reconditioned Volkswagen Touran engines are cleaned and have worn components replaced; rebuilt units undergo full strip-down with new pistons, rings and bearings for maximum longevity.
keyPoints: Reconditioned: £1,200-£3,500, suitable for moderate wear scenarios
Rebuilt: £1,500-£3,800, recommended for high-mileage or catastrophic failure
Both include minimum 12-month warranty through EngineMarket network
Rebuilt units include upgraded parts addressing known Touran failure points
items.cta: Compare reconditioned vs rebuilt Volkswagen Touran engine prices

items.question: Does Volkswagen Touran engine replacement include ECU coding?
items.answer: Yes, all supply & fit Volkswagen Touran engine replacements through our network include necessary ECU coding, adaptation and post-fit diagnostics to ensure proper operation and error code clearance.
keyPoints: ECU coding essential for immobiliser and emissions system compatibility
Included in supply & fit service pricing, not supply-only
Specialists use VCDS/ODIS tools for Volkswagen Group vehicles
Road test and adaptation drive cycle completed before handover
items.cta: Find Volkswagen Touran engine supply & fit specialists

items.question: Are used Volkswagen Touran engines a good option?
items.answer: Used Volkswagen Touran engines from £500-£1,500 offer budget replacement for older vehicles, but verify mileage, compression and service history as warranty coverage is typically shorter than rebuilt units.
keyPoints: BKC/BXE 1.9 TDI most sought-after used engines for reliability
Always request compression test results and service records
Confirm warranty terms - typically 3-6 months vs 12 months on rebuilt
Best value for Tourans under £3,000 where rebuilt cost approaches vehicle value
items.cta: Check used Volkswagen Touran engine availability

items.question: How quickly can I get a Volkswagen Touran engine fitted?
items.answer: Most Volkswagen Touran engine replacements can be completed within 5-7 days from order to road-ready, with next-day engine dispatch available on common codes like BKC, BKD and CAYC from UK stock.
keyPoints: Common engine codes often available for next-day dispatch
Fitting typically requires 1-2 days at specialist garage
Supply & fit service coordinates engine delivery and garage booking
Urgent breakdown support available through network for stranded vehicles
items.cta: Get urgent Volkswagen Touran engine replacement quote
